rulings.Case Title: FIRST RESOLUTION INVESTMENT CORPORATION,SUCCESS VS. WENDERSON ALEXANDRINO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 28, 2011, Line 1. PLAINTIFF FIRST RESOLUTION INVESTMENT CORPORATION, SUCCESS OR IN INTEREST TO PALISADES ACQUISITION LLC'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Order. Off calendar. The moving party failed to provide courtesy copies in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (JJM/302)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE CHANG VS. JANE L. O'HARA GAMP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Respopnses To Discoveyr And Imposition Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 28, 2011, Line 4. DEFENDANTS JANE GAMP AND SAN FRANCISCO LAW SCHOOL'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovey And Imposition Of Sanctions. The motion to compel is granted. All objections overruled with the exception of the attorney work product objections raised in response to Defendant's requests for production numbers 1 - 35. Within 21 days, Plaintiff shall serve full and complete responses to Defendant's interrogatories, produce all non-privileged documents and a complete privilege log with respect to those documents as to which the attorney work product doctrine is asserted. Defendant's motion for sanctions is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to pay $1,200 in sanctions to Defendant within 21 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (JJM/302)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER THOMPSON VS. ROI W. YOUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration And Ntc Of Motion To Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 28, 2011, Line 6. DEFENDANT ROI YOUNG'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration And Notice Of Motion To Stay Proceedings. The motion to compel arbitration is granted and this action is stayed. Plaintiff to seek arbitration and AAA to determine the location arbitration will be conducted pursuant to AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules Rule R-10. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (JJM/302)
